$comment: Cluster F item F1 (C 6.5.2.4 / 6.5.3.1): ++/-- (pre + post, integer + pointer) end-to-end. (1) PRE vs POST distinct: x=5; a=x++ -> a=5,x=6; b=++x -> b=7,x=7. (2) POINTER step sizeof-scaled (the silent-miscompile fix): int arr[3]={10,20,30}; int* p=arr; p++ -> *p==20 (a 1-byte step would read inside arr[0]). (3) PREFIX pointer ++q ALSO scaled -> *q==20. (4) SINGLE-EVALUATION: acc[side()]++ calls side() exactly once (calls==1) and increments acc[0] 1->2. main returns `bad`, which is 0 iff every behavior is correct; ANY F1 regression sets a distinct bit and flips the exit. RED-ON-DISABLE: without the pointer-scaling step, *p/*q read byte offsets inside arr[0] (not 20) -> bad gains bits 8/16; a pre/post yield swap flips bits 1/2/4. The optimizedPipelines `release` arm proves the flow survives the shipped optimizer (the un-inlined side() keeps the complex-lvalue path live). arm64 runs under qemu; macho on the macos-latest leg.
